definition: alpha; the first letter of the Greek alphabet. Used as a symbol for the first (or beginning).
love, affection, good will, benevolence; brotherly love. In a spiritual sense: divine love, the love of God for man and of man for God.
a word, speech, discourse, saying, utterance; reason, account, reckoning; the Word (of God), divine expression. In John's Gospel: Christ as the eternal Word of God.
faith, belief, trust, confidence; fidelity, faithfulness. The conviction that God exists and is the creator and ruler of all things.
grace, favor, kindness, goodwill; the merciful kindness of God; the divine influence upon the heart and its reflection in the life.
beginning, first, chief, firstfruits, first part; the first in time, place, order, or rank.
God, god, gods, rulers, judges; the supreme God; angelic beings; human rulers or judges.
YHWH, Yahweh, Jehovah; the proper name of the God of Israel; the self-existent, eternal God.
